key_insight: Interpretable deep learning can classify fracture causes in zirconia-toughened alumina using low-magnification SEM images.
engineering_relevance: This advancement could streamline quality assurance processes for ceramic implants, enhancing patient safety.
method: Curated a dataset of 8,493 SEM images from various tests.
Employed a vision-transformer (ViT) model to classify fracture causes across three defect categories.
Utilized Grad-CAM for visual explanation of model predictions.
evidence_signal: The fine-tuned ViT achieved an accuracy of 0.907 and a macro-F1 score of 0.888 under severe class imbalance.
watch_item: The model's performance may vary with different materials or manufacturing processes not represented in the original dataset.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
